summary: Codex uber-skill-creator review of the Uber skill pack. All skills pass basic quick validation. All skills with package lints and tests pass them. The earlier deep-rca hardening gap has been closed with Codex metadata, golden evals, package lint, tests, and removal of the package-local README.

cases.prompt: Validate all Uber skill folders with the portable skill quick validator.
cases.expected: Every SKILL.md has valid frontmatter, a valid name, and a valid description.
cases.with_skill: All nine folders passed quick_validate.py: deep-rca, uber-skill-creator, uberaccept, uberassess, ubergoal, uberplan, ubershow, ubersimplify, and uberskillevolver.
cases.without_skill: Not run; this review used the repo-local Codex uber-skill-creator validator as the structural baseline.
cases.notes: Command used /Users/rob/repos/agentic-uber-skills/uber-skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py through uv with pyyaml.

cases.prompt: Review deep-rca as a portable skill package.
cases.expected: Codex metadata, eval/trigger examples, and at least small validation coverage for a reusable skill.
cases.with_skill: deep-rca now passes quick validation, package lint, and tests. It has agents/openai.yaml, golden trigger/non-trigger evals, a package lint, unit tests, and no package-local README.
cases.without_skill: A basic markdown-only review would likely miss the pack-shape gap because the skill text itself is strong.
cases.notes: Hardening completed after the initial review finding.

cases.prompt: Review the new repo-local uber-skill-creator as a general Uber skill creator.
cases.expected: portable wording, provenance boundary, eval-driven extension, report generator, installability, and tests.
cases.with_skill: uber-skill-creator passed quick validation, package lint, and its tests. It has portable wording, provenance notice, eval-driven extension, HTML report generator, openai metadata, and installed symlink coverage.
cases.without_skill: The prior hidden system skill was not repo-owned and did not include the repo-local eval-driven extension.
cases.notes: Residual risk: it has no golden trigger evals of its own yet; add held-out trigger/non-trigger examples after first real use.

cases.prompt: Review ubergoal for thin-wrapper behavior and refactor-campaign usability.
cases.expected: Thin lifecycle router, not a monolith; routes to focused phase skills and captures refactor-campaign profile without requiring long repeated prompts.
cases.with_skill: ubergoal passed quick validation, package lint, and tests. It remains a thin lifecycle wrapper with refactor-campaign profile wiring and goal-objective validation.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.
cases.notes: Continue watching for wrapper bloat as refactor-campaign behavior matures.

cases.prompt: Review uberplan for Codex general planning quality.
cases.expected: Checkable PRD/task map, thin harness/fat agent rubric, source-convention check, acceptance goals, and validators.
cases.with_skill: uberplan passed quick validation, package lint, and 16 tests. It now validates PRD checklist, Mermaid task map, thin-harness rubric, source-convention check, regex/keyword semantic gate, and evidence layers.
cases.without_skill: Prior planning relied more on prose expectations and would be easier for future agents to skip.
cases.notes: This is the strongest current example of uber-skill-creator methodology turned into executable gates.

cases.prompt: Review uberaccept for final acceptance quality.
cases.expected: Adversarial acceptance, architecture drift checks, boundary checks, topology checks, and validators.
cases.with_skill: uberaccept passed quick validation, package lint, and 14 tests. It has final acceptance and architecture-steward validators with negative fixtures.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.

cases.expected: Assessment packets, no-implementation boundary, project fit, source resolution, and approval separation.
cases.with_skill: uberassess passed quick validation, package lint, and 9 tests. Its description and validators preserve assessment vs implementation boundaries.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.

cases.prompt: Review ubershow as a Codex utility skill.
cases.expected: Visual artifacts only when useful, source authority preserved, static templates, receipt mechanism, and tests.
cases.with_skill: ubershow passed quick validation, package lint, and 6 tests. It has templates, schema, snippets, and copyable decision receipt guidance.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.
cases.notes: Implicit invocation is acceptable because it is a task-specific utility, not an Uber lifecycle phase.

cases.expected: Audit-first behavior, patch authorization gates, rollback evidence, dynamic-reference safety, and tests.
cases.with_skill: ubersimplify passed quick validation, package lint, and 12 tests. It has strong report validators and negative fixtures.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.

cases.expected: Evidence-backed learning, no silent self-modification, promotion batches, validators, and tests.
cases.with_skill: uberskillevolver passed quick validation, package lint, and 6 tests. It validates learning records and promotion batches.
cases.without_skill: No separate baseline run; evidence is package lint/tests plus SKILL.md review.

description_tuning.new: No immediate broad trigger-description rewrite recommended. deep-rca hardening is complete; add held-out trigger/non-trigger evals for uber-skill-creator after first real use.
held_out_examples: Use deep-rca to explain why this repeated CI failure keeps coming back.
Create a general portable skill for reviewing Terraform modules.
Run a codebase refactor campaign overnight and generate a report.
Make an HTML decision board for these architecture options.
Assess whether this GitHub repo is worth adopting before we implement anything.
